topic.name: Veterans Health Care Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative measures aimed at improving health care access and services for veterans.

Document 1: Eliminates the requirement limiting reimbursement for emergency treatment in a non-Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) facility to veterans who are active participants in the VA's health care system.

Document 3: Requires the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) to furnish, within 14 days after receiving a request, any record in its custody pertaining to a claim, or relating to the military service or medical records of a veteran.
If the VA is unable to furnish a record by such time, it shall inform the requester within 10 days after receiving the request of the reason for the delay.
Document 4: Requires the U.S. Postal Service to provide for the issuance and sale of a veterans health care stamp to help fund the medical care and treatment of veterans.
Document 5: Reduces the level of difficulty required in the use of prostheses by veterans suffering the loss of hands, arms, or legs as a result of military service.

Document 7: Directs the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) to furnish to a veteran enrolled in the VA's patient enrollment system a drug or medicine that a duly licensed physician has determined such veteran is taking or has taken.
Directs the VA to revise its prescription medication formulary to ensure that all medications within the classes of antipsychotics and antidepressants are available at all VA pharmacies.
Document 8: Requires the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) to reimburse a non-VA entity for health care services provided to a veteran under the Veterans Choice Program.
Document 9: Authorizes the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to cover the costs associated with the care of veterans at medical foster homes.
Document 10: Directs the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) to enter into contracts or agreements with eligible providers to furnish hospital care and medical services to electing, eligible veterans.

The Veterans Access, Choice, and Accountability Act of 2014 is amended to direct the VA to annually transfer to the VHA an amount estimated to be needed to furnish hospital care, medical services, and other health care through non-VA providers.

topic.name: Data Privacy and Security Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at enhancing the protection of personally identifiable information and addressing security breaches in electronic data systems.

Document 1: One Hour Notification Act of 2013 - Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to notify individuals within one hour after a breach of personally identifiable information.
Requires the Secretary to report to Congress annually on all such breaches that occurred within the past year.
Document 2: Location Privacy Protection Act of 2011 - Prohibits the collection of geolocation information without express authorization.
Authorizes civil actions by the Attorney General for violations of this Act.
Document 3: Cyber Privacy Fortification Act of 2013 - Provides criminal penalties for failures to notify of a security breach involving sensitive personally identifiable information.

Document 4: Access to Congressionally Mandated Reports Act - Requires the Government Publishing Office to provide public access to congressionally mandated reports.

Document 5: Personal Data Privacy and Security Act of 2014 - Enhances punishment for identity theft and violations of data privacy.
Imposes criminal penalties for concealing a security breach.
Document 6: Personal Data Protection and Breach Accountability Act of 2011 - Imposes penalties for concealing security breaches involving sensitive personally identifiable information.
Makes it unlawful for service providers to manipulate data without consent.
Document 7: Privacy and Cybercrime Enforcement Act of 2007 - Amends federal criminal code to include computer fraud in racketeering activity.
Requires federal agencies to prepare privacy impact assessments.
Document 8: Personal Tax Information Protection Act - Makes unauthorized disclosure of personally identifiable information unlawful for government employees.
Liability for individuals who aid or abet such violations.
Document 9: Personal Health Information Protection Act - Prohibits unauthorized disclosure of protected health information by government employees.
Liability for violations related to personally identifiable information.
Document 10: Health Exchange Security and Transparency Act of 2015 - Requires notification of individuals within two business days after a security breach.
